item.title: CPA inaugurates two-week training on industrial safety
item.description: The Chittagong Port Authority and the European Union-funded Global Ports Safety inaugurated a two-weeks training course titled Regional Training Course on Industrial Safety at the training centre of Chittagong Port on Monday....

item.title: Eliminate child labour, minister urges orgs
item.description: Labour and employment minister Ariful Haque Choudhury on Monday said that child labour must be eliminated from the country through practical and sustainable projects and the work of eliminating child labour must start from home....

item.title: ‘Within and Beyond’ under way at Safiuddin Shilpalay
item.description: A 10-day exhibition—the fourth solo art showcase by visual artist Masudur Rahman, titled Within and Beyond—is under way at Safiuddin Shilpalay in Dhanmondi, Dhaka....

item.title: GAU honours two Independence Award recipients
item.description: Gazipur Agricultural University on Monday honoured two recipients of the 2026 Independence Award Professor ZA Karim and Professor MA Rahim in recognition of their outstanding contributions to science, education and agricultural research....

item.title: Reopening of state-owned jute mills demanded
item.description: Politicians, academics and labour leaders on Monday, at a discussion meeting in Dhaka, called on the Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government to reopen the closed state-owned jute mills of the country....

item.title: DU passes Tk 1,033cr budget with zero research allocation
item.description: The Dhaka University senate on Monday approved a Tk 1033.22 crore budget for the financial year 2026-27 with no funds dedicated to research....

item.title: Venezuela teams scramble as hope fades of finding quake survivors
item.description: Hopes were fading Monday of finding survivors more than four days after powerful twin earthquakes struck Venezuela, as residents grow increasingly frustrated with the government’s response to the disaster that has killed at least 1,450 people and left tens of thousands unaccounted for....

item.title: Journalist Azahar Ali arrested in fresh case
item.description: A Dhaka court on Monday was granted permission to show journalist Azahar Ali Sarker arrested in a fresh case under the Anti-Terrorism Act....

item.title: Youth’s eyes gouged out over theft allegation
item.description: A group of people gouged out the eyes of a youth in a village in Sylhet on Sunday evening, suspecting him of being a thief....

item.title: Shikha Anirban goes out
item.description: The Shikha Anirban, a memorial symbolising enduring courage and supreme sacrifices of the armed forces during the during the 1971 Liberation War located in Dhaka Cantonment, has now been kept mostly off since May 2 as part of the government’s austerity measures to save energy....

item.title: New govt body to manage banks’ distressed assets on the card
item.description: The government has decided to establish a Distressed Asset Management Unit as a fully independent statutory body to tackle the banking sector’s massive stock of distressed assets....

item.title: JS scraps untaxed income legalisation
item.description: The Jatiya Sangsad on Monday passed the finance bill 2026 with some major changes, including the revocation of the provision of voluntary declaration and legalisation of undisclosed income amid criticisms that it will benefit tax evaders in the new financial year of 2026-27, beginning from tomorrow....

item.title: Continued detention after bail order risks rule of law
item.description: IT IS deeply disparaging that the arbitrary detention and abuse of the law that were rampant under the fallen Awami League regime continue unabated. Many lawyers have reported in recent months that prison authorities and the police have frequently prevented the execution of bail orders based solely on communications from Advocates-on-Record at the Attorney......Business& Industrial

item.title: Eradicating child labour must be national priority
item.description: THE failure to eliminate child labour by the government’s own deadlines is a shame. The authorities set targets to eliminate the worst forms of child labour by 2021 and all forms of child labour by 2025, and have missed both. The latest Bangladesh Bureau of Statistics survey on child labour reveals that 17.8 lakh children remain trapped in labour, with 10.7 lakh engaged in......

item.title: Cry for electricity
item.description: EVERY summer in Bangladesh has its own vocabulary. We learn to speak through heat indexes, weather bulletins, rising electricity demand and promises of improvement. Yet beyond the language of megawatts lies another language that rarely enters official briefings. It is the language of sleepless nights, interrupted livelihoods, spoiled medicines, dying poultry, failed......

item.title: Bangladesh, a river civilisation
item.description: BANGLADESH was not born from stone. Bangladesh was born from water. Every year, rivers break this land apart, and those same rivers build new land again. People sow seeds again. They build homes again. They sing again. They love again. They begin again. This rebirth is Bangladesh’s first identity. The true culture of Bangladesh was not born in a palace. It was not......

item.title: Dhaka’s urban crisis: roadmap for decentralisation
item.description: DHAKA ranks among the world’s worst cities in which to live. The air is toxic. Traffic is gridlocked. Infrastructure is failing. This is no secret. Scholars and policymakers have documented these problems for decades. What is missing is not analysis. What is missing is action......Politics(Right)

item.title: Politics of institutional silence
item.description: PUBLIC institutions exist to serve citizens, not merely to administer them. Their legitimacy depends not only on how they perform their duties but also on how openly they account for them. Yet in Bangladesh, a journalist approaching a government office or autonomous institution with a camera is often met with a familiar response from the very official appointed to communicate with the public: ‘Speaking on camera is not possible.’......

item.title: Spain confident despite injury setbacks
item.description: Spain remain confident of winning the World Cup, defender Marcos Llorente said, despite a series of injuries that have ravaged La Roja's options in wide areas....

item.title: Sweden ready for 'game of our lives' against France
item.description: Sweden coach Graham Potter captured the size of the task awaiting his side when they play France at the World Cup on Tuesday as he admitted they will have to ‘play the best game we have ever played’ to beat one of the tournament favourites....

item.title: Coach says Japan getting closer to World Cup glory despite defeat
item.description: Coach Hajime Moriyasu said Japan have proved they have what it takes to one day win the World Cup following a last-gasp 2-1 defeat to Brazil on Monday....

item.title: Ancelotti never doubted 'suffering' Brazil would score
item.description:
Brazil coach Carlo Ancelotti said he knew his ‘suffering’ side would eventually score and praised their patience in reaching the World Cup last 16 with a last-gasp 2-1 win over Japan on Monday.
...

item.title: Last-gasp Martinelli sends Brazil into last 16
item.description: Substitute Gabriel Martinelli scored deep in injury time as Brazil beat a stubborn Japan 2-1 on Monday to roll into the World Cup last 16....
